---
pagetitle: "CV – Elly Kirwa"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

```{=html}
<nav aria-label="breadcrumb">
  <ol class="breadcrumb">
    <li class="breadcrumb-item"><a href="index.html">Home</a></li>
    <li class="breadcrumb-item active" aria-current="page">CV</li>
  </ol>
</nav>

<div style="display:flex; gap:1rem; margin-bottom:2rem; flex-wrap:wrap;">
  <a href="misc/info/Elly_Kirwa_CV.pdf" download style="
    display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;
    background:#1e567d; color:#fff; padding:10px 20px;
    border-radius:6px; text-decoration:none; font-weight:600;">
    <i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download CV (PDF)
  </a>
  <a href="misc/info/Elly_Kirwa_Resume.pdf" download style="
    display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem;
    background:#28a745; color:#fff; padding:10px 20px;
    border-radius:6px; text-decoration:none; font-weight:600;">
    <i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download Resume (PDF)
  </a>
</div>
```

## Professional Summary

Veterinary epidemiologist and data scientist with 5+ years of experience applying statistical analysis and machine learning to complex health and production data. Experienced in building efficient data pipelines, developing interactive tools, and translating analytical results into actionable insights that support improved animal health, operational performance, and profitability.

---

## Education

**Ph.D. in Population Sciences in Animal Health** `Aug 2023 – Spring 2027 (Expected)`
Iowa State University (ISU), Ames, IA
*Dissertation: "Applied Data Analytics for Breeding Herd Efficiency in Commercial Swine Production"*
Graduate Certificate: Business Data Analytics (Jan 2026 – Dec 2026)

**M.Sc. in Veterinary Preventive Medicine** `Aug 2023 – Dec 2025`
Iowa State University (ISU), Ames, IA

**M.Sc. in Pharmacology & Toxicology** `Sep 2018 – Sep 2022`
University of Nairobi, Nairobi, Kenya

**Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM)** `Sep 2012 – Sep 2017`
University of Nairobi, Nairobi, Kenya — *With Distinction*

---

## Skills

- **Programming:** R, Python, SQL, SAS
- **Machine Learning:** Supervised/Unsupervised Learning, Deep Learning, Natural Language Processing
- **Data Engineering:** Feature Engineering, Large-Scale Data Processing, Data Cleaning
- **Epidemiology:** Epidemiological studies and clinical trials
- **Cloud Services:** Microsoft Azure, DuckDB, AWS
- **Molecular Techniques:** PCR, DNA extraction, whole genome sequencing
- **Toxicology:** HPLC, gas chromatography (GC), spectroscopy
- **Clifton Strengths:** Strategic, Learner, Achiever, Responsibility

---

## Professional Experience

**Data Analytics Research Assistant** `Aug 2023 – Present`
*Iowa State University | Ames, IA*

- Precision Swine Management: Designing and implementing projects focused on developing / applying algorithms to improve herd efficiency
- Data Analytics: Utilizing Python, R, and SAS for complex analysis of sow-level production data and sequenced data to develop benchmark performance dashboards and disease surveillance platforms
- Field Trials: Designing and executing field trials assessing the impact of vaccination programs

**Veterinarian & Research Manager** `June 2018 – May 2023`
*Stanley & Son Limited | Salama, Kenya*

- Data-Driven Production: Introduced production-guided research and data analysis to integrate evidence-based production efficiency, including feed quality testing and somatic cell count monitoring through a developed Farm Management System
- Antimicrobial Stewardship: Developed an antimicrobial surveillance platform, including a policy framework for antimicrobial use in animals and residues in milk, to ensure farm-level food safety
- Clinical & Management: Managed all veterinary and animal husbandry requirements, including diagnosis, surgery, post-mortems, disease control programs, and staff supervision

**Veterinary Intern** `June 2017 – May 2018`
*Africa Network for Animal Welfare | Nairobi, Kenya*

- Attended wildlife veterinary emergencies, rescues, treatments, field surgeries, and desnaring in various National Parks and Conservancies
- Neutered ~330 animals in spay/neuter campaigns in partnership with local governments and VetTeks-USA
- Rabies control through vaccinations and spay/neuter programs
- Reviewed Animal Welfare laws in Kenya for consideration in a Parliamentary motion

**OHCEA Workforce** `June – July 2017`
*One Health East and Central Africa / USAID | Kenya*

- Executed demo site workforce on community needs assessment and data collection among the Maasai Community in Loitokitok, Kenya
- Educated the pastoral community on better livestock production methods and zoonotic diseases
- Developed a workforce guide model for use in various One Health demo sites

**Product Development Officer (Part-Time)** `May 2015 – Dec 2022`
*Obulex Solutions Limited | Westlands, Nairobi*

- Assisted in the development and testing of software including Revenue Information Management System (RMIS) for Isiolo and Mombasa Counties, and Afya Poa Telemedicine App
- Graphics design and website development for various clients

---

## Awards & Fellowships

- **Real Pork Scholar (2025)** — National Pork Board, USA
- **Best Oral PhD Presentation** — ASAS Midwest Conference, USA (2025)
- **Mandela Washington Fellow (MWF)** (2023)
- MSc Fellowship/Scholarship, University of Nairobi (2017)
- MSc Scholarship, Iowa State University (2023)
- PhD Assistantship/Scholarship, Iowa State University (2023)
- **Best Overall Student**, DVM Class of 2016/2017
- DVM Exchange Program Recipient, Free University of Berlin, Germany (2015)
- Undergraduate Fellowship, University of Nairobi: One Health Research Project (2012–2017)

---

## Peer-Reviewed Publications

1. **Kirwa, E.**, Peterson, B., Grohmann, C. J., Silva, G. S., Ulguim, R. R., Mellagi, A. P., & Frizzo, M. (2025). Assessment of the relationship between lactation feeding patterns, litter performance measures, and sow characteristics on sow farrowing efficiency metrics. *Journal of Animal Science*, 103(Supplement_1), 50–52. [doi:10.1093/jas/skaf102.055](https://doi.org/10.1093/jas/skaf102.055)

2. Musskopf, M.; Peterson, T.; Machado, I.; Tran Pham Tien, T.; **Kirwa, E.**; et al. (2025). Assessment of the Relationship Between Bioexclusion Practices Applied in Wean-to-Harvest Sites and PRRS Outbreaks. *Vet. Sci.* 12, 1000. [doi:10.3390/vetsci12101000](https://doi.org/10.3390/vetsci12101000)

3. **Kirwa E**, Tangen J., Vargas L.B., Musskopf M., et al. (2025). Dynamics of PRRSV Seroconversion and immune-modulating properties of tilmicosin during naive gilt exposure to PRRS LVI.

4. **Kirwa, E.**, Aboge G. O., Maitho, T. E., Mbindyo, C. M., Abuom, T. O., & Mainga, A. O. (2021). Antibiotic profile of *Staphylococcus aureus* and Coagulase negative *Staphylococci* from raw camel milk from Garissa County, Kenya. *East African Journal of Science, Technology and Innovation*, 2(4). [doi:10.37425/eajsti.v2i4.364](https://doi.org/10.37425/eajsti.v2i4.364)

5. Bahati D., Makau D., **Kirwa E.** & Mutura J. (2019). An assessment on community perspectives and views on natural resource management and its implications to biodiversity conservation. [doi:10.13140/RG.2.2.10344.34566](http://dx.doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.10344.34566)

---

## Selected Presentations & Abstracts

1. **Kirwa EK**, et al. Predicting Farrowing Success to Inform Breeding Decisions in Commercial Swine Herds. *Symposium on AI in Veterinary Medicine*, Ithaca, NY. May 2026. Oral.
2. **Kirwa EK**, et al. Leveraging Machine Learning to Predict Subsequent Farrowing Success. *Society of Animal Science Midwest*, Omaha. March 2026. Oral.
3. **Kirwa EK**, et al. Factors associated with gilt retention in breeding herds. *Society of Animal Science*, Omaha. March 2026. Oral.
4. **Kirwa EK**, et al. Using Field Data to Identify Drivers of Gilt Retention and Lifetime Productivity. *The Robust Pig Seminar, PIC*. Feb 2026.
5. **Kirwa EK**, et al. Practical strategies to maximize sow efficiency. *National Hog Farmer*, 2025. [Link](https://www.nationalhogfarmer.com/livestock-management/practical-strategies-to-maximize-sow-efficiency)
6. **Kirwa EK**, et al. Linking lactation feeding patterns, litter performance, and sow characteristics to sow efficiency metrics. *Benchmark Magazine*, Spring 2025, pp. 6–7. [Link](https://www.pigchamp.com/flipbooks/benchmark-magazine/2025/USA/4/index.html)
7. **Kirwa EK**, et al. Factors associated with gilt retention. *International Conference on Pig Livability*, Omaha. 2025. Oral.
8. **Kirwa EK**: One Health Concept — The Kenyan Perspective. *Mandela Washington Fellowship Conference*, Minneapolis. 2023.
9. **Kirwa EK**, et al. Phenotypic characterization of antimicrobial-resistant *S. aureus*. *ESCMID/ASM Conference*, Dublin. 2022. Poster.
10. **Kirwa EK**: How safe is our milk on the shelf? *Kenya One Health Conference*, ILRI, Kenya. 2021.

---

## Grant Funding

**Awarded**

- Silva, Portal, Rosero, **Kirwa**: Real-time surveillance system for mortality in wean-to-finish lots — *Pig Livability*: **$30,000**
- Silva, Ulgium, **Kirwa**: Early Identification of High-Risk Piglets in Commercial Swine Systems — *Pig Livability*: **$30,000**
- Silva, Linhares, Holtkamp, Rosero, Trevisan, Rupasinghe, **Kirwa**: Precision sow management system (PSMS) — *ISU VPR*: **$50,000**

**Under Review**

- Silva, Linhares, Holtkamp, Rosero, Trevisan, Rupasinghe, **Kirwa**: Improving breeding herd efficiency by unravelling predictors of sow retention, productivity and profitability — *USDA-CARE*: $300,000

---

## Leadership & Community Engagement

- **Moderator**, Weekly Swine Seminar — VetMed, Iowa State University (Dec 2025 – Present)
- **IVSA Officer**, Kenyan Chapter (Jan 2016 – Sep 2017)
- **Assistant Secretary**, One Health Club — University of Nairobi (March 2014 – May 2018)
- **Member**, Kenyan Animal Welfare Advocacy Network (Dec 2016 – May 2017)

**Professional Memberships:** Association of American Swine Veterinarians (AASV) · American Society of Animal Science · Toxicology Society of Kenya · Kenya Small and Companion Animal Veterinary Association · Kenya Veterinary Association · Kenya Society for the Protection and Care of Animals
